title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 21 - Mini-Bosses and Bosses
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9hOkPAYmpPM

Daniel (@dungeondive) surveys the big threats of Shadows of Brimstone—the bosses and mini-bosses that anchor campaign climaxes and hex crawl boss hunts. The Jorogumo spider queen wields spider magic and rolls eight combat dice with web-spinning abilities. Hari Nago floats on animated hair that damages every hero within three spaces. The Gastro Tyrant sprays acid templates and lays egg clutches in the Belly of the Beast. The Ancient One (Cthulhu itself) wields the Sword of Felgnar, reshapes reality each turn via a random chart, and carries a health pool of 40+20 per hero—explicitly not recommended for level-one parties.

The Sand Kraken features a soulless eye mechanic (heroes can target it to bypass defence but risk sanity damage on a miss). The Burrower is a massive void worm with entirely variable movement and attacks. Belial, Lord of Cinder, redeems the Caverns of Cynder as a chained shadow king with fiery magic and a destructible key around his neck. Finally, Sho Ryu the Dragon King commands elemental dragon traits and dragon magic with a health pool of 60+10 per hero. Daniel teases a future episode on setting up a boss-hunt hex crawl campaign.

----

Which boss would you want as the final encounter of a hex crawl campaign—Belial, the Ancient One, or Sho Ryu?
